req.title: Account statement generation (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall request monthly statements as PDF; statements older than 7 years shall be fetched from cold storage. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) normal PDF delivery for a recent authenticated month, (2) cold-storage retrieval for statements older than 7 years, (3) 404 when no activity exists in the requested period, (4) 503 when cold storage is unavailable, and (5) 401/403 for unauthorised callers. Equivalence Partitioning separates active-period vs cold-storage vs empty-period buckets. BVA targets the exact 7-year boundary (84 months). NEG covers the unauthorised-caller and cold-storage-unavailable paths. DT covers the combined decision of period age x activity presence x cold-storage health. STC models the retrieval lifecycle state machine (REQUESTED -> FETCHED/ERROR). The violation in the original response was test[4] having kind=negative but heuristic=STC; STC is a state-transition heuristic appropriate for modelling state-machine paths and is not inherently negative. The fix reassigns that test to heuristic=NEG (which correctly classifies the unauthorised-access negative path) and introduces a separate STC test modelling the valid state transition REQUESTED -> FETCHED to satisfy the STC heuristic with a positive kind, preserving all original tests.

tests.name: test_statement_pdf_delivered_for_recent_active_month
preconditions: Target month has at least one transaction
Month is within the last 7 years (hot storage)
actions: GET /statements/{month} with a month within the last 12 months
Include valid Bearer token in Authorization header
expected: Response body is non-empty binary PDF
X-Retrieval-Logged header present or audit log entry created
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import authenticated_client, recent_active_month

def test_statement_pdf_delivered_for_recent_active_month(authenticated_client, recent_active_month):
    month = recent_active_month  # e.g. '2024-11'
    resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/statements/{month}")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.headers["Content-Type"] == "application/pdf"
    assert len(resp.content) > 0
    assert resp.headers.get("X-Retrieval-Logged") == "true" or <helper_fn>.audit_log_has_entry(month)

tests.name: test_statement_404_when_no_activity_in_period
preconditions: Target month has zero transactions (inactive period)
actions: GET /statements/{month} with a month known to have no activity
Include valid Bearer token in Authorization header
expected: Response body contains error code indicating no activity
trace: R-FIN-058: 404 if no activity in period
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import authenticated_client, inactive_month

def test_statement_404_when_no_activity_in_period(authenticated_client, inactive_month):
    month = inactive_month  # e.g. '2023-02' seeded with no transactions
    resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/statements/{month}")

    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") is not None
    assert "pdf" not in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")

tests.name: test_statement_retrieval_at_exact_7_year_boundary
preconditions: Statement exists for the month exactly 84 months ago (7 * 12)
actions: Compute target month as today minus exactly 84 months
Include valid Bearer token in Authorization header
expected: Response body is non-empty binary PDF (fetched from cold storage)
trace: R-FIN-058: statements older than 7 years fetched from cold storage
tests.executable:
import pytest
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta
from datetime import date
from <fixture> import authenticated_client, ensure_cold_storage_statement

def test_statement_retrieval_at_exact_7_year_boundary(authenticated_client, ensure_cold_storage_statement):
    boundary_month = (date.today() - relativedelta(months=84)).strftime("%Y-%m")
    ensure_cold_storage_statement(boundary_month)

    resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/statements/{boundary_month}")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.headers["Content-Type"] == "application/pdf"
    assert len(resp.content) > 0
    log = <helper_fn>.get_last_audit_entry(boundary_month)
    assert log["source"] == "cold_storage"

tests.name: test_statement_503_when_cold_storage_unavailable
preconditions: Target month is older than 7 years
Cold storage service is simulated as unavailable (mocked 503)
actions: Mock cold storage dependency to return 503
GET /statements/{month} with a month older than 7 years
Include valid Bearer token in Authorization header
expected: Response body contains error indicating cold storage unavailability
tests.executable:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch
from <fixture> import authenticated_client

def test_statement_503_when_cold_storage_unavailable(authenticated_client):
    old_month = "2015-06"  # deterministically older than 7 years
    with patch("<app_module>.cold_storage.fetch", side_effect=<helper_fn>.cold_storage_unavailable_error()):
        resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/statements/{old_month}")

    assert resp.status_code == 503
    body = resp.json()
    assert "cold_storage" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    assert "pdf" not in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")

tests.name: test_statement_401_when_caller_is_not_account_owner
preconditions: A valid token belonging to a DIFFERENT customer is used
actions: GET /statements/{month} using another customer's valid Bearer token
Target account belongs to a different owner
expected: No retrieval log entry created for the unauthorised attempt
trace: NEG: ownership mismatch must be rejected before any storage access
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import other_customer_client, active_month_for_account_a

def test_statement_401_when_caller_is_not_account_owner(other_customer_client, active_month_for_account_a):
    # other_customer_client is authenticated as customer B trying account A's month
    month = active_month_for_account_a
    resp = other_customer_client.get(f"/statements/{month}")

    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403)
    assert "pdf" not in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    assert not <helper_fn>.audit_log_has_entry(month, caller="customer_b")

tests.name: test_statement_one_month_before_7_year_boundary_uses_hot_storage
preconditions: Statement exists for exactly 83 months ago (one month inside 7-year window)
actions: Compute target month as today minus exactly 83 months
Include valid Bearer token in Authorization header
expected: Response body is non-empty binary PDF
trace: R-FIN-058: BVA month inside 7-year boundary served from hot storage
tests.executable:
import pytest
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta
from datetime import date
from <fixture> import authenticated_client, ensure_hot_storage_statement

def test_statement_one_month_before_7_year_boundary_uses_hot_storage(authenticated_client, ensure_hot_storage_statement):
    inside_month = (date.today() - relativedelta(months=83)).strftime("%Y-%m")
    ensure_hot_storage_statement(inside_month)

    resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/statements/{inside_month}")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.headers["Content-Type"] == "application/pdf"
    assert len(resp.content) > 0
    log = <helper_fn>.get_last_audit_entry(inside_month)
    assert log["source"] == "hot_storage"

tests.name: test_statement_retrieval_state_transition_requested_to_fetched
preconditions: Target month has at least one transaction
Retrieval state machine starts in REQUESTED state
actions: GET /statements/{month} with a valid recent month
Include valid Bearer token in Authorization header
Observe state transitions via audit log or response metadata
expected: Audit log records state transition from REQUESTED to FETCHED
Response body is non-empty binary PDF
trace: STC: state REQUESTED -> FETCHED (successful retrieval path)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import authenticated_client, recent_active_month

def test_statement_retrieval_state_transition_requested_to_fetched(authenticated_client, recent_active_month):
    month = recent_active_month
    resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/statements/{month}")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.headers["Content-Type"] == "application/pdf"
    assert len(resp.content) > 0
    log = <helper_fn>.get_last_audit_entry(month)
    assert log["initial_state"] == "REQUESTED"
    assert log["final_state"] == "FETCHED"
